def: n. m. Dim. kuiyāṅ , n. f. कूप A well. 1. Mūṅh sūī, peṭ kūī . Prov. His mouth is like the eye of a needle, but his belly is a well. (Small morsels, large consumption). 2. Idhar girūṅ kūāṅ, udhar girūṅ khāī . Prov. Or fall here in a well, or there into a ditch. 3. kūe kī miṭṭī kūe hī meṅ lag jātī hai . Prov. The earth dug for a well upon the well is used. (The profits of the mill are spent on the mill). 4. Kahe se koī kue meṅ nahīṅ gir paṛtā . Prov. One does not fall into a well because he is told to. 5. ' Kuëṅ meṅ kyā?' 'Mainā.' 'Yār māṅge so denā .' Boys' rhyme. A form of speech by which juvenile friendships are cemented. 6. Kuāṅ bechā hai, kueṅ kā pānī nahīṅ bechā . Prov. I've sold the well, not the water. 7. Kuiyāṅ ke jaisan kāī, ham tū oise janme ke bhāī . Bhoj. Boys. As green scum to the well doth cleave we two as brothers own shall live. kuāṅ pūjnā . The Hin. ceremony of worshipping a well at the birth of a son or marriage. kuāṅ ṭūṭnā , v. n. To break, or be exhausted (a well). kuāṅ jotnā yā chalānā , v. a. To irrigate from a well. kuāṅ chhoṛnā , v. a. To stop drawing water from a well. kūāṅ khodnā , v. a. 1. To dig a well. 2. To dig a pit for another. khārī kūāṅ . A well of brackish water. mīṭhā kūāṅ . A well of which the water is sweet. kūoṅ meṅ bāṅs ḍālnā , v. a. To make a thorough search. kue jhakānā , v. a. 1. To make one look into seven wells (a supposed cure for Hydrophobia). 2. To lead one by the nose; lead one a pretty dance. Zarā chāh kā dekh apnī mazā, jhakātī hūṅ kaise kue maiṅ bhalā! Mīr Hasan. A leman's love you would enjoy, would you? I'll give you a taste, seven wells I'll make you view. kue meṅ bolnā , v. n. To speak in a low tone.
s.m. A well; a pit

ety: prob. a caus. form. fr. S. कुट्ट; or akin to kaćūrnā, q.v.

v. a. To bruise, to crush.
subs: or kućal-ḍālnā, v.t. intens. of and = kućalnā.

ety: from old H. कछू = S. कच्चित् (akin to किंचित्)
def: pron. & adv. Something, somewhat, anything, aught; some, any; a little, a few; ever so little; whatever; in any manner or degree, at all
subs: Somewhat more; something additional; something different, a very different tale or account; a false account or explanation
Somewhat, some little, a little, some few
It is nothing, it is a matter of no consequence, there is nothing in this; (this) is nothing to the purpose, (this) is no reason at all; (this) is no excuse, (this) will not do or answer
It is of no consequence, no matter, never mind, nʼimporte
Have you found something lying (in your path)ʼ? have you found a treasure? (addressed to one who seems inordinately pleased without apparent cause)

'What! have you dreamt something?' or What! have you seen a vision? Are you in your senses? (addressed to a person who relates improbabilities; or used to express the astonishment of the speaker at any unexpected act of another's)
There is no fear, or there is nothing to fear, do not be alarmed! it does not matter, no matter, never mind
Something else, something quite different; (in) another manner; on the contrary; — a great advance or increase (on)
or kućh-se kućh ho jānā, To become quite different, to be or become transformed, to be or become entirely changed
Some little, a little, somewhat; rather; tolerably
v.t. To make something else of, to transform; to bewitch, charm
or kućh kah-baiṭhnā (-se), To say something unpleasant (to)
Some at least, somewhat; something or other
Nothing, not any, none; it is nothing at all; it is of no account
Whatever may be, happen or come what may; — kućh ho-jānā, To be quite changed, to be completely transformed; to be possessed by an evil spirit
Everything, the whole, all that was possible

ety: kūd˚ = Prk. कुंद(इ) = S. स्कुन्द(ते), rt. स्कुन्द्
def: v.n. To leap, jump, spring, bound; to hop; to hobble; — (met.) to rejoice; to boast; to presume (upon, ūpar)

See اُچهل کود kūdte phirnā , v. a. 1. To frisk about. 2. To wander idly about. kudā'ī , n. f. 1. Jumping into water. 2. A present made on this account.

def: s.f. Power, ability, potency, vigour, force, authority, virtue; divine power, omnipotence; — the creation, the universe, nature
more: n. f. 1. Divine power. Teri qudrat ke āge koi zor kīsī kā chale nahīṅ, Chīṅṭī par hāthī chaṛh baiṭhe tab wo chīṅṭī mare nahīṅ . Banārsī. Against thy strength, O Lord! all strength is vain, 'Neath tread of elephant the ant may safe remain. 2. Creation; the universe; nature; cosmos. qudrat rakhnā, qādir h. , v. n. To have power; to be able. qudrat-i-kāmilah . Omnipotence.
(from قدر) s. f. 1. Power, authority. 2. Omnipotence.
subs: (-kī), To have power (to), to be able.

s.f. Weak sight, defective vision; evil eye; ill-intended look; lascivious glance; — a false system.

def: s.m. A padlock, a lock; a bolt
